name: kalahar_stones
description: The Reach Remembers (Boundary Stones), q.side_kalahar_stones slot 43, end-to-end: accept Warden Ossa's survey at the records post, dig the two dune-line stones (seal_stone_1/_2), clear the guarded third stone on the Old Caravan Road via the CITE fork (150 CD pay-probe, cite_surveyor.mcfunction -> stone3_guard_clear), pry the two-seal rubbing (seal_stone_3), file the counter-claim (file_claim: 600-face payout + npc_gift/kalahar_ground + kalahar_claim_filed + boundary_stones_done), then read the filed tombstone + granary hook. NO BATTLE BY DESIGN: the wager fight (sq_boundary_surveyor, lv 50-51, above-cap) is render-asserted only, never clicked - mystic_mirror precedent, the button rendering IS the surface and clicking it exposes the known intermittent TBCS battle-init stall. Both cite forks are exercised (declined at 100 CD, accepted at 900 CD); balance asserts sit under 1000 because cobbledollars query abbreviates >= 1000 (safari_yards precedent). Movement is tp-based (mystic_mirror precedent) - the quest surface is dialog/tag routing, not proximity latches. Fresh player: no prereq tags (Ossa's accept is open by default).

steps.comment: -- SITE NOTES: all four tp targets are integer-authored PROPOSED placements (character files say builder-confirm in-world) - the Y values are unverified, listed in _probe_pending; ground-probe live before the first run. Stones 1+2 share the display name Basalt Survey Stone but sit ~170b apart, far outside the 48b find radius, so nearest-match find_npc is safe at each site. --

steps.comment_: leftover-dialog guard: a prior aborted run can leave a dialog up; closing with no screen open is a no-op

steps.comment: -- reset (idempotent re-runs): every tag the chain sets + the keepsake items. No kills / latch resets needed - all five bodies are pure dialog props whose state lives on the player. Defensive: grain_factor_kalahar (ON_DISTANCE_VERY_CLOSE ambush when the wheat traders are hostile) stands ~17b from stone_1's dune-line site, so the hostility tags AND their driving score are cleared (wheat_war_first_farm precedent). --

steps.comment_: a campaign player at fields_liberated >= 4 re-derives wheat_trader_hostile next tick and the grain factor beside stone_1 would force a DOUBLES ambush
instant band-tag removal so we do not race the band tick
generous funding for the fee rail; the cite beat pins exact sub-1000 values later (query abbreviates >= 1000)

steps.comment: -- beat 1: Warden Ossa at the records post - the accept. say[] rolls one line at random per open --

steps.comment_: latch spawn on first visit + band_tags re-derives the no_* inverses stripped above

expect_screen_text: Ossa. I keep the old land records - what the dunes have not eaten. The Company holds title to the northern fields. Their title is paper.

The real boundary was cut into three basalt survey stones a lifetime ago - common land, all of it - then the sand took them. Unearth the three and I can file a counter-claim that the granary they seized was never theirs to sell. Walk the dune line. The stones remember.

steps.click: I will dig up the stones

steps.comment: -- beat 2: stone 1 at the dune line - dig + rubbing --

steps.expect_screen_text: A basalt slab, most of it under the dune. Brush it clear and a boundary mark shows - older than any Company charter. Ossa will want a rubbing of this.

steps.click: Clear the sand and take a rubbing

steps.comment: -- beat 3: stone 2 at the dune edge east - same dig pattern --

steps.expect_screen_text: A second basalt slab, half-swallowed by the dune. The same old mark runs across it - common land, all of it. Ossa will want this rubbing too.

steps.click: Clear the sand and take a rubbing

steps.comment: -- beat 4: the guarded stone on the Old Caravan Road. First the DECLINED cite fork: balance 100 sits under the 150 CD filing fee, so cite_surveyor's pay-probe must fail-soft - no charge, no tag. The wager button is render-asserted only (see description). --

expect_screen_text: A Land Surveyor from the Company stands squarely on the third stone, clipboard out, boots on the boundary mark. He informs you this stretch is surveyed, verified, and closed pending review.
You can fight him for the ground, or read his own boundary law back at him and watch the clipboard win the argument for you.

steps.expect_screen_text: Wager battle - 400 CD purse, 200 CD if you lose, above the cap
steps.comment_: render-assert ONLY, never clicked: the wager starts a TBCS battle exposed to the known intermittent battle-init stall, and the fight sits above-cap by design; the cite fork is this scenario's mainline

steps.click: Cite Ossas counter-charter back at him - 150 CD filing fee

steps.comment: -- the FUNDED cite: 900 CD -> fee 150 lands, balance 750, the surveyor stands down (stone3_guard_clear) --

steps.expect_screen_text: Cite Ossas counter-charter back at him - 150 CD filing fee

steps.click: Cite Ossas counter-charter back at him - 150 CD filing fee

steps.wait_chat: The surveyor reads his own boundary law

steps.comment_: the cd_calc probe + charge + tag land, then band_tags before the re-open

steps.comment: -- the pry: cleared entry (prio 25) preempts the guard, the rubbing sets seal_stone_3 --

steps.expect_screen_text: The surveyor has stepped aside. The stone is clear to read. Under the sand: two seals, one older than the Company itself.

steps.comment: -- beat 5: back to Ossa - the two-seal recognition deflect (return_stone3, prio 30) + the filing. file_claim fires subtitle THEN title; wait_chat is seq-cursored, so the subtitle is waited first --

expect_screen_text: You brought all three rubbings. Common land, marked and dated - the Company title is paper over paper.

This third stone. It bears two seals. The Company mark, yes. But under it, older, a personal cut - and I have seen that cut on exactly one charter in my life. The founders own hand. And it is the same stroke as the way you just signed my log. Funny thing, the desert. It keeps what people bury.
No. I am an old woman reading marks in bad light. Let it lie. Take your reward and my thanks.

steps.wait_chat: ADJUSTMENT: rounding, in the Company favor
steps.comment_: receipt sting fires on the real turn-in only, before the tombstone tag latches

steps.comment: -- the keepsake bundle (npc_gift/kalahar_ground): 1x hard stone + 2x soft sand + 4x sand, exact counts - the preamble cleared all three ids. The 600-face payout is instability-skewed, so no exact balance assert (miller_walk precedent: items, not coin). --
-- beat 6: the filed tombstone (prio 40) + the Crossroads Granary liberation hook. No buttons on this entry - close via the driver --

expect_screen_text: Three stones, three rubbings, one counter-claim filed in ink older than the Company. The northern fields were common land. Paper says so now, and paper is testimony.
If you find yourself out by the Crossroads Granary - it was never theirs to hold. Someone should go take it back. I filed the reason; you can be the argument.

steps.comment_: the tombstone entry has no buttons

steps.comment: -- cleanup: strip everything the chain latched so no other scenario inherits it (Marisols stones_button gates on not boundary_stones_done; kalahar_claim_filed is a farm_5 liberation callback). Keepsakes cleared; the balance rides at ~750 + the skewed payout (the fee rail has no reset, miller_walk precedent). --
